name: ANSYS Fluent
description: Industry-leading computational fluid dynamics (CFD) software for simulating combustion, aerodynamics, and thermal flows. Used to design rocket engine injectors, nozzles, aerodynamic fairings, and re-entry vehicles.
whyUseIt: Rocket engines involve the most extreme fluid dynamics on Earth — supersonic combustion at 3,500K, cryogenic propellant flow, and shock-boundary layer interactions. ANSYS Fluent handles all of these with validated solvers that aerospace companies trust for flight certification.

vocabulary.definition: Computational Fluid Dynamics — numerically solving the Navier-Stokes equations to predict fluid flow
Mathematical model that approximates the effects of turbulent flow (k-epsilon, k-omega SST, etc.)

beginnerMistakes: Insufficient mesh resolution in boundary layers
Using the wrong turbulence model for the flow regime
Not validating CFD results against experimental data
